ICtoolkit 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Compute RBIC for model objects

Source: R/compute_rbic.R
compute_rbic.Rd

Computes the Regularized Bayesian Information Criterion (RBIC), an extension of EBIC that accounts for structured (grouped) predictors. RBIC applies group-specific EBIC penalties:

compute_rbic(fit, P_index, gamma = "ebic", ...)

Arguments

fit

A fitted model object of class lm, glm, glmnet, or ncvreg.

P_index

A named list of character vectors defining variable groups. See the P_index format section.

gamma

Gamma specification. See the gamma argument section. Default "ebic".

...

Currently unused; reserved for future use.

Value

A named numeric scalar (lm/glm) or vector (glmnet/ncvreg) with the following attributes:

fit_class

Character. The primary class of fit.

k

Integer scalar or vector. Total estimated parameters (including intercept).

gamma

The computed gamma value(s): a scalar or vector for lm/glm; a list (one entry per lambda) for glmnet/ncvreg.

P_index

The P_index supplied by the user.

criterion

"RBIC".

Details

$$\mathrm{RBIC} = \mathrm{BIC} + 2 \sum_{g=1}^{G} \gamma_g \log\binom{P_g}{k_g}$$

where G is the number of groups, P_g is the total number of candidate predictors in group g, k_g is the number selected from group g, and gamma_g is a group-level tuning parameter.

P_index format

P_index is a named list whose elements identify which predictors belong to each group:

  • lm/glm: character vectors of term labels as returned by attr(terms(fit), "term.labels") (e.g. "x1", "I(x1^2)", "x1:x2").

  • glmnet: character vectors matching rownames(fit$beta).

  • ncvreg: character vectors matching rownames(fit$beta[-1, ]).

The gamma argument

gamma controls how the group-level tuning parameters are determined. Accepted values:

"ebic" (default)

Single gamma based on the total number of candidates P_total = sum(p_1, ..., p_K), applied uniformly across all groups. Matches the paper's default formula: $$\gamma = \frac{\log(P_{total}/\sqrt{n})}{\log P_{total}} \cdot \mathbf{1}(P_{total} \ge \sqrt{n})$$

"per_group"

Separate gamma for each group, each computed from its own p_k. Stronger penalisation of larger groups.

A numeric scalar

Fixed gamma applied uniformly to all groups, e.g. gamma = 0.5.

A numeric vector of length K

Fixed group-specific gammas.

A function f(P, k, n)

Advanced: called once with the full vectors of group sizes and selected counts; must return a scalar or length-K vector.

References

Chen, J., & Chen, Z. (2008). Extended Bayesian information criteria for model selection with large model spaces. Biometrika, 95(3), 759–771.

See also

compute_ebic(), compute_bic(), ic_step()

Examples

set.seed(1)
n  <- 100
x1 <- rnorm(n); x2 <- rnorm(n); x3 <- rnorm(n)
y  <- 2 * x1 - x2 + rnorm(n)
df <- data.frame(y, x1, x2, x3, x1sq = x1^2, x2sq = x2^2)
fit <- lm(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x1sq + x2sq, data = df)

P_index <- list(main = c("x1", "x2", "x3"), squared = c("x1sq", "x2sq"))

compute_rbic(fit, P_index)                          # default (total-P gamma)
#> [1] 303.5027
#> attr(,"fit_class")
#> [1] "lm"
#> attr(,"k")
#> [1] 6
#> attr(,"criterion")
#> [1] "RBIC"
#> attr(,"gamma")
#> [1] 0
#> attr(,"P_index")
#> attr(,"P_index")$main
#> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3"
#> 
#> attr(,"P_index")$squared
#> [1] "x1sq" "x2sq"
#> 
compute_rbic(fit, P_index, gamma = "per_group")     # per-group gammas
#> [1] 303.5027
#> attr(,"fit_class")
#> [1] "lm"
#> attr(,"k")
#> [1] 6
#> attr(,"criterion")
#> [1] "RBIC"
#> attr(,"gamma")
#>    main squared 
#>       0       0 
#> attr(,"P_index")
#> attr(,"P_index")$main
#> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3"
#> 
#> attr(,"P_index")$squared
#> [1] "x1sq" "x2sq"
#> 
compute_rbic(fit, P_index, gamma = 0.5)             # fixed gamma
#> [1] 303.5027
#> attr(,"fit_class")
#> [1] "lm"
#> attr(,"k")
#> [1] 6
#> attr(,"criterion")
#> [1] "RBIC"
#> attr(,"gamma")
#> [1] 0.5
#> attr(,"P_index")
#> attr(,"P_index")$main
#> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3"
#> 
#> attr(,"P_index")$squared
#> [1] "x1sq" "x2sq"
#> 
compute_rbic(fit, P_index, gamma = 0)               # gamma=0 recovers BIC
#> [1] 303.5027
#> attr(,"fit_class")
#> [1] "lm"
#> attr(,"k")
#> [1] 6
#> attr(,"criterion")
#> [1] "RBIC"
#> attr(,"gamma")
#> [1] 0
#> attr(,"P_index")
#> attr(,"P_index")$main
#> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3"
#> 
#> attr(,"P_index")$squared
#> [1] "x1sq" "x2sq"
#>
